messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0128",
  "title": "2025年全球热门地区手游营销观察",
  "page_index": 71,
  "page_range": "71"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0129",
    "title": "2025 GLOBAL MOBILE GAME MARKETING TREND INSIGHTS 2025年全球热门地区手游营销观察06",
    "page_range": "71-72",
    "summary": "This parent section delivers a comprehensive, region-by-region analysis of 2025 global mobile game advertising trends, supplemented by industry expert commentary on market evolution and technological disruption. Covering the reporting period of January to December 2025, the content is structured to support retrieval queries on regional market scale, creative format dominance, top-performing titles, monthly campaign fluctuations, and strategic industry shifts.\n\n**Topics & Metrics:** The section quantifies year-over-year growth for active advertisers and deduplicated ad creatives across eight regions: North America (+56% advertisers/7.1W, +67% creatives/14.2M), Japan & South Korea (+51%/61K, +55%/13.4M), China HK/Macau/TW (+26%/111K, +42%/19.7M), Southeast Asia (+52%/68K, +69%/11.7M), Middle East (+58%/21.1W, +54%/29.9M), Europe (+22%/67K, +34%/12.8M), Oceania (+40%/11.8W, +76%/19.6M), and South America (monthly advertiser volumes scaling 0–40K). Video creatives dominate globally, accounting for 70.2%–77.7% of ad formats depending on the region. Monthly tracking includes advertiser counts, active creative volumes, and new-entry ratios.\n\n**Claims & Trends:** Key claims highlight regional strategic divergences: Europe shows slow growth with a pivot toward puzzle/casual genres and niche IP integration; South America demonstrates a consistent upward trend in creative refresh rates (new creative adoption rising from 66.0% in January to 83.8% in December); Southeast Asia notes Middle East titles peaking in May/June with slow iteration cycles (<40% new creative ratio); and HK/Macau/TW exhibits the lowest global video share but high acceptance of Chinese, Japanese, and Korean local IPs. Android leads globally in advertiser and creative volume.\n\n**Entities & Products:** Trending..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0143",
    "title": "2025 GLOBAL MOBILE GAME MARKETING TREND INSIGHTS 2025年手游热门广告主营销洞察07",
    "page_range": "85",
    "summary": "This section serves as a title/header for a report segment focused on 2025 global mobile game marketing trends, specifically targeting marketing insights for popular mobile game advertisers. It can answer high-level contextual questions regarding the document's primary topic (global mobile game marketing), relevant entities (mobile game advertisers, global gaming market), and timeframe (2025). The provided excerpt contains no substantive definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, metrics, or evidence types, and therefore cannot answer detailed analytical, procedural, or data-driven questions. Retrieval queries should target this section only for thematic identification, document navigation, or metadata context related to 2025 mobile game advertising trends."
  }
]

message.content: This parent section delivers a comprehensive, region-by-region analysis of 2025 global mobile game advertising trends and popular advertiser marketing insights, covering the reporting period of January to December 2025. It addresses topics including regional market scale, creative format dominance, top-performing titles, monthly campaign fluctuations, strategic industry shifts, market evolution, and technological disruption. Key entities include mobile game advertisers, the global gaming market, eight target regions (North America, Japan & South Korea, China HK/Macau/TW, Southeast Asia, Middle East, Europe, Oceania, and South America), the Android platform, and specific genre/IP categories (puzzle/casual, niche IPs, and Chinese/Japanese/Korean local IPs). The section presents quantitative metrics such as year-over-year growth rates and absolute volumes for active advertisers and deduplicated ad creatives across all eight regions (e.g., North America +56%/7.1W advertisers, +67%/14.2M creatives; South America monthly advertiser volumes scaling 0–40K), global video creative dominance (70.2%–77.7%), monthly advertiser counts, active creative volumes, and new-entry ratios. Specific claims highlight regional strategic divergences: Europe shows slow growth with a pivot toward puzzle/casual genres and niche IP integration; South America demonstrates a consistent upward trend in creative refresh rates (new creative adoption rising from 66.0% in January to 83.8% in December); Southeast Asia notes Middle East titles peaking in May/June with slow iteration cycles (<40% new creative ratio); and China HK/Macau/TW exhibits the lowest global video share but high acceptance of regional IPs, while Android leads globally in advertiser and creative volume. Evidence types consist of quantitative regional tracking data, monthly campaign metrics, and industry expert commentary. No tables, figures, definitions, or procedures are explicitly detailed in the provided excerpts. This content supports retrieval queries on 2025 mobile game advertising benchmarks, regional performance comparisons, and advertiser strategy shifts.
